name: layer-pruning
paperTitle: On the Limits of Layer Pruning for Generative Reasoning in LLMs
content:
## Title & Introduction

Not all layers contribute equally to reasoning. This skill teaches how to identify and prune less critical layers while maintaining model capabilities.

## Core Concept

Effective layer pruning requires careful analysis of layer importance for different tasks.

## Implementation

```python
class LayerPruner:
    def compute_layer_importance(self, model, val_data):
        importance = {}
        for name, layer in model.named_modules():
            # Measure impact of removing this layer
            importance[name] = self.measure_layer_criticality(model, layer, val_data)
        return importance

    def prune_layers(self, model, importance_scores, target_ratio=0.9):
        # Keep only most important layers
        threshold = np.percentile(list(importance_scores.values()), 100 * (1 - target_ratio))
        pruned_layers = {name: layer for name, layer in model.named_modules() 
                        if importance_scores.get(name, 0) >= threshold}
        return pruned_layers
```

## Practical Guidance

- Start with 20-30% reduction target
- Validate on reasoning tasks specifically
- Avoid pruning early transformer layers (important for encoding)

## Reference

Layer pruning works best when applied selectively based on task-specific importance analysis.
